title: Maluti temples
tagline: Maluti temples — spiritual town of Jharkhand

overview.short: Maluti temples are a group of 72 extant terracotta temples (out of the original number of 108), located in the Maluti village near Shikaripara in Dumka district on the eastern part
overview.description: Maluti temples are a group of 72 extant terracotta temples (out of the original number of 108), located in the Maluti village near Shikaripara in Dumka district on the eastern part of the Chota Nagpur Plateau, Indian state of Jharkhand. These temples, according to the Indian Trust for Rural Heritage and Development (ITRHD), were built between the 17th and 19th centuries. The kings of Baj Basanta dynasty built these temples in Maluti, their capital, inspired by goddess Mowlakshi, their family deity. topPlaces.name: Maluti
topPlaces.description: Maluti (also Malooti) is a village in Shikaripara CD block in the Dumka subdivision of the Dumka district of Jharkhand, India. It was built under the Baj Basanta dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Ulpahari
topPlaces.description: Ulpahari is a tribal village in Birbhum district, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Chakmandala
topPlaces.description: Chakmandala is a village in Rampurhat I CD Block in Rampurhat subdivision of Birbhum district in the Indian state of West Bengal

topPlaces.name: Mallarpur
topPlaces.description: Mallarpur is a census town in Mayureswar I CD Block in Rampurhat subdivision of Birbhum district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Madian, Birbhum
topPlaces.description: Madian is a village in Mayureswar I CD block in Rampurhat subdivision of Birbhum district.

topPlaces.name: Gonpur
topPlaces.description: Gonpur (also spelled Ganpur) is an old village in Mohammad Bazar CD Block in Suri Sadar subdivision of Birbhum district, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Sarasdangal
topPlaces.description: Sarasdangal is a village situated in the Dumka District in Jharkhand State, Eastern India. It is situated on the Dumka - Rampurhat Bus Route.

topPlaces.name: Fatehpur, Birbhum
topPlaces.description: Fatehpur is a census town and a neighbourhood of Mallarpur town in Mayureswar I CD block of Rampurhat subdivision in Birbhum district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maluti temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maluti temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maluti temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (184 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (132 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maluti temples?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maluti temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maluti temples?
faq.a: Stays in Maluti temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maluti temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Maluti, Ulpahari, Chakmandala, Mallarpur, Madian, Birbhum.
